title: potentilla procumbens subsp. italica
intro: Potentilla procumbens subsp. italica, also known as Italian creeping cinquefoil, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Rosaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant activity, which may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its safety and efficacy for medicinal use.
